---
title: "Bilingual Assholes: Ratings Graphs"
author: "morgan moyer"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(ggplot2)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(multcomp) # not available for this version of R
library(stringr)
library(textstem)
library(sciplot)
library(tidyverse)
theme_set(theme_bw())
cbPalette <- c("#56B4E9", "#D55E00", "#009E73","#999999", "#E69F00")
vaPalette <- c("#FCC6BB", "#F54927", "#9C2007", "#440E03")
gPal <- c("#B9E615","#5E15E6","#F78D02", "#009E73")

# scale_fill_manual(values = vaPalette)
# valPal <- c("#F4320B")

this.dir <- dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path)
setwd(this.dir)
source("../helpers.R")

d <- read.csv("../data/processed_rating.csv")
pairs <- read.csv("../data/word_pairs.csv")

length(unique(d$Word))
nrow(d)

```


## Summary Stats
```{r}
agr <- d %>% 
  group_by(Word) %>% 
  summarize(MeanValence = mean(likert_valence),
            V.SD = sd(likert_valence),
            MeanArousal = mean(likert_arousal),
            A.SD = sd(likert_arousal))

```

```{r}

hist(d$likert_valence,
     # breaks = seq(0.5, 5, 9),  # centers bars on integers (for 1–7 scale)
     main = "Histogram of Likert Responses",
     xlab = "Likert Score",
     col = "lightblue")


counts <- table(d$Language,d$likert_valence)

barplot(counts,
        main = "Distribution of Likert Responses",
        xlab = "Likert Score",
        ylab = "Frequency",
        col = "lightblue")


ggplot(d, aes(x = factor(likert_valence))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "lightblue") +
  facet_wrap(~Language) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Likert Responses",
       x = "Likert Score",
       y = "Count")


ggplot(d, aes(x = factor(likert_arousal))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "lightblue") +
  facet_wrap(~Language) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Likert Responses",
       x = "Likert Score",
       y = "Count")

```


```{r}
names(d)

agr <- d %>% 
  group_by(Word,Language) %>% 
  summarize(MeanValence = mean(likert_valence),
            SD = sd(likert_valence),
            MeanArousal = mean(likert_arousal),
            SD = sd(likert_arousal))
  
# dev.off()  # Close all devices
ggplot(agr, aes(x = MeanArousal, y = MeanValence, color = Language)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = Word), 
                  max.overlaps = Inf,
                  box.padding = 0.3,
                  point.padding = 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Italian" = "#F54927", "English" = "#440E03")) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 9), ylim = c(0, 9)) +  # Better than xlim/ylim
  labs(x = "Likert Arousal", y = "Likert Valence",
       # title = "Word Valence vs Arousal Ratings",
       color = "Language") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )

```


# Looking at word pairs


```{r}
agr <- d %>%
  group_by(Word,Language) %>% 
  summarize(MeanValence = mean(likert_valence),
            SD = sd(likert_valence),
            MeanArousal = mean(likert_arousal),
            SD = sd(likert_arousal))

View(pairs)
result <- pairs %>%
  # join English rows
  left_join(
    agr %>% filter(Language == "English"),
    by = c("English" = "Word")
  ) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(., "_EN"),
              c(MeanValence, SD, MeanArousal)) %>%
  # join Italian rows
  left_join(
    agr %>% filter(Language == "Italian"),
    by = c("Italian" = "Word")
  ) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(., "_IT"),
              c(MeanValence, SD, MeanArousal))

# result <- result %>% tidyr::drop_na()
names(results)
View(results)
results <- results %>% 
  mutate(MeanValDifference = abs(MeanValence_EN - MeanValence_IT),
         MeanAroDifference = abs(MeanArousal_EN - MeanArousal_IT))

```
